title: Cabinet-Style Farmhouse Nightstand with Drawer
summary: A fully enclosed farmhouse nightstand — plywood cabinet box, one drawer, and a solid door below it, distinct from this catalog's open-frame nightstands.
description:
Where this catalog's other nightstands are open frame-and-shelf builds, this one is a fully enclosed cabinet box: solid plywood sides, top, and bottom joined into a case, a single drawer up top, and a hinged door below that closes off the lower storage completely. Build the case first as one square, glued-and-screwed plywood box, and confirm it's square before hanging the door — a case that's slightly out of parallel telegraphs directly into a door that won't sit flush.

The drawer rides on simple wood runners screwed to the case sides, sized for a comfortably loose fit rather than a precision slide — appropriate for a bedside drawer's light duty holding glasses, a book, or a charging cable. The door is a simple flat panel hung on two hinges with a magnetic catch, no face frame needed since the case sides themselves form the door's stopped edge.

A 1x2 face trim around the drawer and door openings dresses up the plywood edges and gives the piece a furniture-grade look rather than a bare-plywood one — this trim also doubles as the door's stop on the hinge side.

tools.note: Breaking down the plywood case parts.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the case
steps.body: Cut the case sides, top, bottom, and fixed shelf. Drill pocket holes and assemble into a square box with glue and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Attach the plywood back.

steps.title: Add the face trim
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 face trim around the case opening edges with brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build and install the drawer
steps.body: Build the drawer box with glue and brad nails, attach the bottom, then screw in the wood drawer runners inside the case. Attach the drawer front and pull.

steps.title: Hang the door
steps.body: Attach the hinges to the door panel and to the case, check for even reveal on all sides, then install the magnetic catch and door pull.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces, then prime and paint or stain and seal.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Enclosed cabinet-style farmhouse nightstand with a drawer and door
